response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said presents a genuinely polyglot, long-tenured senior IC with credible high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby) and very recent AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP tooling. However, Python does not appear anywhere in the resume — not in skills, not in project descriptions, not in employment history — and it is listed as a must-have for this role. The AI/agent infrastructure exposure is limited to roughly one month at the current employer, making depth hard to assess. TypeScript and Node.js are clearly strong, distributed systems work exists but is six years old, and there is no evidence of on-call ownership or incident-response experience. The profile warrants a short exploratory screen to probe Python fluency and operational maturity before committing to a full loop.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, aligning with the AI-native engineering environment requirement
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level IC track record across multiple employers with evidence of ownership mindset (took over iOS dev at WatchUp, founded and runs GISCollective open-source platform)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from skills, project descriptions, and employment history — a stated must-have for this role with no mitigating signals anywhere in the resume
AI/agent infrastructure experience appears to be less than two months old; depth and complexity of that work cannot be assessed from the resume alone
No mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or production operational ownership, which is a mandatory non-technical requirement for this position

key_factors.factor: AI-agent / MCP infrastructure at current role
